Name: Heracross
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It loves sweet nectar. To keep all the nectar to itself, it hurls rivals away with its prized horn.

Name: Burmy
Flavor-Text: To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

Name: Mothim
Flavor-Text: It loves the honey of flowers and steals honey collected by Combee.

Name: Combee
Flavor-Text: At night, Combee sleep in a group of about a hundred, packed closely together in a lump.

Name: Vespiquen
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It houses its colony in cells in its body and releases various pheromones to make those grubs do its bidding.

Name: Cherubi
Flavor-Text: It nimbly dashes about to avoid getting pecked by bird Pokémon that would love to make off with its small, nutrient-rich storage ball.

Name: Cherrim
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: As a bud, it barely moves. It sits still, placidly waiting for sunlight to appear.

Name: Cherrim
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: After absorbing plenty of sunlight, Cherrim takes this form. It’s full of energy while it’s like this, and its liveliness will go on until sundown.

Name: Carnivine
Ability-Effect: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, attacks used by this Pokémon do +30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.

Name: Leafeon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may take a [G] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your [G] Pokémon.

Name: Houndour
Flavor-Text: It is smart enough to hunt in packs. It uses a variety of cries for communicating with others.

Name: Houndoom
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text: If you are burned by the flames it shoots from its mouth, the pain will never go away.

Name: Heatran
Ability-Effect: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

Name: Marill
Flavor-Text: The fur on its body naturally repels water. It can stay dry even when it plays in the water.

Name: Azumarill
Flavor-Text: Its long ears are superb sensors. It can distinguish the movements of things in water and tell what they are.

Name: Barboach
Flavor-Text: Its two whiskers provide a sensitive radar. Even in muddy waters, it can detect its prey’s location.

Name: Whiscash
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It is extremely protective of its territory. If any foe approaches, it attacks using vicious tremors.

Name: Snorunt
Flavor-Text: It is said that several Snorunt gather under giant leaves and live together in harmony.

Name: Froslass
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it finds humans or Pokémon it likes, it freezes them and takes them to its chilly den, where they become decorations.

Name: Snover
Flavor-Text: During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain’s lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.

Name: Abomasnow
Ability-Effect: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, attacks used by this Pokémon cost 1 less [C] Energy.
Flavor-Text: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.

Name: Glaceon ex
Ability-Effect: During Pokémon Checkup, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, do 10 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Origin Forme Palkia
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It soars across the sky in a form that greatly resembles the creator of all things. Perhaps this imitation of appearance is Palkia’s strategy for gaining Arceus’s powers.

Name: Phione
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: When the water warms, they inflate the flotation sac on their heads and drift languidly on the sea in packs.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

Name: Raichu
Ability-Effect: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

Name: Electrike
Flavor-Text: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

Name: Manectric
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.
Flavor-Text: It stimulates its own muscles with electricity, so it can move quickly. It eases its soreness with electricity, too, so it can recover quickly as well.

Name: Clefairy
Flavor-Text: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

Name: Clefable
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do −30 damage.
Flavor-Text: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

Name: Gastly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent reveals a random card from their hand and shuffles it into their deck.
Flavor-Text: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

Name: Haunter
Flavor-Text: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

Name: Gengar
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: To steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey’s shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.

Name: Unown
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may choose either player. Look at the top card of that player's deck.
Flavor-Text: Its flat, thin body is always stuck on walls. Its shape appears to have some meaning.

Name: Rotom
Ability-Effect: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its electricity-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

Name: Sudowoodo
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Although it always pretends to be a tree, its composition appears more similar to rock than to vegetation.

Name: Phanpy
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives and nests on a riverbank. After playing in the mud, it won’t be able to settle down unless it washes its body.

Name: Donphan
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Rolling Spin attack does +60 damage.
Flavor-Text: Donphan is covered in tough hide, so even being hit by a car won’t faze this Pokémon. However, it is extremely susceptible to rain.

Name: Larvitar
Flavor-Text: Born deep underground, this Pokémon becomes a pupa after eating enough dirt to make a mountain.

Name: Pupitar
Flavor-Text: Its shell is as hard as bedrock, and it is also very strong. Its thrashing can topple a mountain.

Name: Tyranitar
Ability-Effect: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, attacks used by this Pokémon do +30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Extremely strong, it can change the landscape. It is so insolent that it doesn’t care about others.

Name: Nosepass
Flavor-Text: It moves less than an inch a year, but when it’s in a jam, it will spin and drill down into the ground in a split second.

Name: Meditite
Flavor-Text: It never skips its daily yoga training. It heightens its inner strength through meditation.

Name: Medicham
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe’s next move.

Name: Gible
Flavor-Text: It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

Name: Gabite
Flavor-Text: In rare cases, it molts and sheds its scales. Medicine containing its scales as an ingredient will make a weary body feel invigorated.

Name: Garchomp ex
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

Name: Zubat
Flavor-Text: It emits ultrasonic waves from its mouth to check its surroundings. Even in tight caves, Zubat flies around with skill.

Name: Golbat
Flavor-Text: It loves to drink other creatures’ blood. It’s said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it’s gathered.

Name: Crobat
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, you may do 30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Both of its legs have turned into wings. Without a sound, Crobat flies swiftly toward its prey and sinks its fangs into the nape of its target’s neck.

Name: Croagunk
Flavor-Text: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

Name: Toxicroak
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Do 20 damage to this Pokémon instead of the usual amount for this Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: Swaying and dodging the attacks of its foes, it weaves its flexible body in close, then lunges out with its poisonous claws.

Name: Magnemite
Flavor-Text: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

Name: Magneton
Flavor-Text: Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.

Name: Magnezone
Ability-Effect: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

Name: Mawile
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a [M] Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.

Name: Probopass ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

Name: Bronzor
Flavor-Text: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor’s back contained a mysterious power.

Name: Bronzong
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. It was found buried in the ground.

Name: Origin Forme Dialga
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.
Flavor-Text: Radiant light caused Dialga to take on a form bearing a striking resemblance to the creator Pokémon. Dialga now wields such colossal strength that one must conclude this is its true form.

Name: Giratina
Effects: Discard 2 random Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours, where common knowledge is distorted and strange.

Name: Eevee
Flavor-Text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

Name: Snorlax
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

Name: Hoothoot
Flavor-Text: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

Name: Noctowl
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand.
Flavor-Text: Its eyes are specially developed to enable it to see clearly even in murky darkness and minimal light.

Name: Starly
Flavor-Text: They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.

Name: Staravia
Flavor-Text: Recognizing their own weakness, they always live in a group. When alone, a Staravia cries noisily.

Name: Staraptor
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.

Name: Shaymin
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your Active Basic Pokemon's Retreat Cost is 1 less.
Flavor-Text: It can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.

Name: Arceus
Effects: Discard a random Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: According to the legends of Sinnoh, this Pokémon emerged from an egg and shaped all there is in this world.

Name: Arceus ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can't be affected by any Special Conditions.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Irida
Description: Heal 40 damage from each of your Pokémon that has any [W] Energy attached.

Name: Celestic Town Elder
Description: Put 1 random Basic Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.

Name: Barry
Description: During this turn, attacks used by your Snorlax, Heracross, and Staraptor cost 2 less [C] Energy.

Name: Adaman
Description: During your opponent's next turn, all of your [M] Pokémon take −20 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

Name: Houndoom
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text: If you are burned by the flames it shoots from its mouth, the pain will never go away.
